title: symphyotrichum lanceolatum subsp. hesperium
intro: Symphyotrichum lanceolatum subsp. hesperium, commonly known as the narrowleaf coneflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The narrowleaf coneflower is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote wound healing and alleviate respiratory issues.
